codeReview.summary: This fix adequately resolves the original blocking issue from session-cookie-server. The hardcoded "change-me" fallback was removed, startup now fails fast when both SESSION_SECRET and JWT_SECRET are missing, and test infrastructure was updated so server tests still initialize session middleware with an explicit test secret. Added unit tests cover all three secret-handling branches.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission AGENTS auth bootstrap guidance is still inconsistent with the current session-cookie architecture.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/andy/.factory/missions/80c5e196-cca5-4acd-9581-c44e4271690a/AGENTS.md:72 says login returns a JWT token, while the Session Cookie Auth Migration section starts at line 79 and states JWT is being replaced by session cookies.

sharedStateObservations.observation: implementation-worker procedure and compliance signaling are out of sync for TDD ordering.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill requires tests BEFORE implementation (/Users/andy/Documents/GitHub/org-planner/.factory/skills/implementation-worker/SKILL.md:30-34), but this worker transcript skeleton shows a code edit to session.ts before creating sessionSecret.test.ts; handoff still marks followedProcedure=true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Secret-handling guidance does not explicitly prevent reading local .env values into transcripts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 32174885-0a53-405c-a164-035c77cc6e5c includes a Read of packages/server/.env and a tool result echoing env variable values; AGENTS/skill currently warn against committing secrets but do not explicitly warn against transcript/log exposure.

summary: PASS. The rerun fix closes the prior blocking security gap by removing the predictable fallback secret and enforcing fail-fast secret validation at startup, with supporting test-environment setup and targeted unit coverage.
